sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: ACTION7.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 65, level 2.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - Clicking blue circle only absorbs magenta squares that are already close to it - Square at (49-50, 40-41) is still too far to be absorbed - Used 34 actions, target is 42 - Haven't tried ACTION7 yet
- Plan: Try ACTION7 to see what it does. It might be a shortcut or have a different effect.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 35 of about 42 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
ACTION7 had no impact - it only changed the HUD band. So ACTION7 is not useful for solving this level.
I notice that the blue circle's pixel count changed from 69 to 65, and there's now a white object with 2 pixels (W: [2]). Also, the black background changed from 3353 to 3373. This is interesting - something changed even though the magenta positions are the same.
Wait, actually, the board_changed was False, so these changes might be due to the HUD band changing. Let me not worry about these minor changes.
I need to focus on moving the magenta squares to the blue circle. I've used 35 actions out of 42 target. That leaves 7 actions.
Let me think about the most efficient way to move the remaining magenta squares to the blue circle:
- (35, 36, 16, 17) - far left, needs to move up-left to reach blue circle area
- (49, 50, 40, 41) - closer to blue circle, needs to move up-left
- (56, 57, 15, 16) - far left, bottom, needs to move up-left
The blue circle is at rows 23-31, cols 29-37.
For (49-50, 40-41): needs to move up about 18 rows and left about 11 cols to reach the blue circle area. That's about 18 actions just for this square, which is too many.
Wait, maybe I'm overthinking this. Let me reconsider the goal. Maybe the goal is not to move all magenta squares to the blue circle. Maybe the goal is to move just one or two magenta squares to specific positions.
Actually, let me look at the image again. The magenta square inside the blue circle is at (26-27, 32-33). The blue circle is at rows 23-31, cols 29-37. So the magenta square is roughly in the center of the blue circle.
Maybe the goal is to have all magenta squares inside the blue circle at the same time. But that would require moving all 4 squares (excluding the one in the top bar) to the blue circle, which would take many actions.
Alternatively, maybe the goal is to have the magenta squares form a specific pattern. Looking at the current positions:
- (1, 2, 5, 6) - top bar
- (26, 27, 32, 33) - inside blue circle
- (35, 36, 16, 17) - far left
- (49, 50, 40, 41) - moving towards blue circle
- (56, 57, 15, 16) - far left, bottom
Hmm, I don't see an obvious pattern. Let me try a different approach.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the movement mechanic. When I click on a magenta square's top-left cell, it moves up-left by 1 cell. But what if I click on other cells of the magenta square? Maybe it moves in different directions.
Let me test this by clicking on the bottom-right cell of the magenta square at (49-50, 40-41), which would be at (50, 41).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- ACTION7 had no impact, only changed HUD band
- Blue circle pixel count changed slightly (69 to 65), but board_changed was False
- Used 35 actions, target is 42
- Need to find a more efficient way to move magenta squares
Plan: Test if clicking on different cells of magenta square moves it in different directions. Also continue moving the square at (49-50, 40-41) towards the blue circle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test clicking on different cells of magenta square at (49-50, 40-41)
# Try clicking on bottom-right cell (50, 41)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 41}])

after = current_frame.segmentation
m_positions = []
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        r = (min([p[0] for p in b]), max([p[0] for p in b]), min([p[1] for p in b]), max([p[1] for p in b]))
        m_positions.append(r)

print(f"Magenta positions: {sorted(m_positions)}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

# If the square moved, check which direction
if m_positions != [(1, 2, 5, 6), (26, 27, 32, 33), (35, 36, 16, 17), (49, 50, 40, 41), (56, 57, 15, 16)]:
    print("Square moved!")
    # Find which square moved
    for mp in m_positions:
        if mp not in [(1, 2, 5, 6), (26, 27, 32, 33), (35, 36, 16, 17), (49, 50, 40, 41), (56, 57, 15, 16)]:
            print(f"New position: {mp}")
else:
    print("Square did not move")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Magenta positions: [(1, 2, 5, 6), (26, 27, 32, 33), (35, 36, 16, 17), (49, 50, 40, 41), (56, 57, 15, 16)]
Board changed: True
Square did not move
